title: TrustLens shared definitions
description: Definitions shared by every TrustLens evidence record. Referenced by finding.schema.json and evidence_record.schema.json.

schema_version.description: Version of the shared evidence schema this record conforms to. See SCHEMA.md for the compatibility rules governing MAJOR/MINOR/PATCH.

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 with an explicit offset. UTC is preferred. A timestamp without an offset is not accepted, because a stale-input warning that cannot be ordered is not a warning.

component.description: Which TrustLens component produced the record or finding.

tool_identity.description: Identity of the code that produced this record. Required for reproducibility: a record whose producing version is unknown cannot be re-derived.

commit.description: Git commit of the working tree that produced this record, or null if not obtainable. Never fabricate a commit.

commit_dirty.description: True if the working tree had uncommitted changes at run time. A dirty tree means the record is not reproducible from the recorded commit alone.

external_tools.description: Third-party analysers invoked for this run, with the versions actually executed. Populated from the tool's own version output, not from a pinned expectation.

invocation.description: Argv actually used, with any local paths preserved so the run can be repeated.

version_source.description: How the version was established. `unknown` is permitted and is more honest than a guess.

artifact_identity.description: What was analysed, where it came from, and how it was obtained.

artifact_id.description: Stable local identifier for this artifact within a TrustLens run set.

declared_kind.description: What the artifact presents itself as (e.g. 'dataset'). Verbatim from its metadata. Null when the artifact makes no such claim — which is itself reportable, and is NOT to be defaulted to 'dataset' or to anything else.

source.description: Original source reference (URL, remote, or local path). Null only when genuinely unknown.

immutable_reference.description: Commit SHA, revision, or content digest pinning the exact state acquired. Null when the source could not supply one — in which case the acquisition is not reproducible and that must be stated in the record's limitations.

acquisition_authorised_by.description: Record of the explicit user initiation for any remote acquisition. Null is only valid when acquisition_method is `already_local` or `user_supplied_path`.

content_hash_method.description: `directory_manifest_v1` = SHA-256 over a sorted newline-delimited list of `<relative_path>\0<sha256>` for every included file. Defined in SCHEMA.md so a third party can recompute it.

excluded_item.description: A path deliberately not analysed, by policy or configuration. Distinct from a failure: an exclusion is a choice, and choices do not invalidate a NOT_FOUND_WITHIN_ANALYSED_SCOPE result — they bound it.

failed_item.description: A path that analysis intended to cover but could not complete. Any non-empty list of these forces PARTIAL. This is the structural mechanism that prevents a parse failure being reported as a clean scan.

reason.description: Concrete reason, including the underlying error text where available. 'error' alone is not an acceptable reason.

scope.description: The exact scope over which a check ran. Every status claim is a claim about THIS scope and nothing wider.

analysed.description: Paths (or node identifiers, for graph components) successfully analysed in full.

languages.description: Languages/formats the check is able to parse. A file in an unlisted language is out of scope even if present in the artifact.

vacuous.description: True when `analysed` is empty — the check ran but had nothing applicable to examine. A vacuous clean result carries no information and MUST be rendered distinctly from a non-vacuous one. Required to be true whenever `analysed` is empty.

evidence_location.description: Where the behavior becomes visible. Evidence is anchored at the point the capability becomes reachable, not at the top of the file.

path.description: Artifact-relative path. Never an absolute host path.

pointer.description: JSON Pointer or dotted key for structured formats.

excerpt.description: Minimal excerpt showing the construct. Bounded so records do not become artifact copies.

redacted.description: True if the excerpt was redacted. TrustLens records the NAMES of credential-shaped variables and paths, never their values; when a value would have appeared it is replaced and this flag set.

detail.description: Free text for locations with no file coordinate, e.g. a pickle opcode offset or a policy statement id.

environment_description_ref.description: Reference to the offline environment description a config-derived or graph-derived record depends on. `description_captured_at` travels with every record and every edge derived from it; it is surfaced in reports rather than kept in metadata, because staleness here is structural rather than incidental.

description_captured_at.description: When the described environment was actually observed — NOT when the file was written or parsed. Supplied by the operator and unverifiable by TrustLens.

captured_at_basis.description: How the capture time was established. `operator_asserted` means a human typed it and TrustLens cannot check it.

unknown_item.description: Something TrustLens could not determine. Recorded positively rather than omitted, so that a report's silence is never mistaken for a clean result.

would_be_resolved_by.description: The concrete input or capability that would turn this unknown into a determination.

unsupported_item.description: A construct the component cannot assess at all.
